commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: Manual review is needed because this external PR is proof-gated and widens a browser file-access boundary that maintainers should explicitly approve before any repair or merge.; Needs attention: The PR broadens the browser upload file-access boundary and needs maintainer approval plus end-to-end proof before merge.; Review finding: [P1] Centralize the inbound upload resolver — extensions/browser/src/browser-tool.ts:... (sha=722ea75eef831ce6c9a74b67cd09e4b30a2cf0e0)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds an inbound media directory fallback to `browser.upload` path normalization and two browser-tool unit tests for fallback acceptance and outside-root rejection.

Reproducibility: yes. Source inspection shows managed inbound media is stored under the config media root while the current browser upload wrapper, server route, and Playwright validators all accept only `DEFAULT_UPLOAD_DIR`; I did not run a live WebChat flow in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: Manual review is needed because this external PR is proof-gated and widens a browser file-access boundary that maintainers should explicitly approve before any repair or merge.

target.title: Fix/browser upload inbound media
target.body:
`browser.upload` rejected files from `~/.openclaw/media/inbound/` because `resolveExistingPathsWithinRoot` only accepted `DEFAULT_UPLOAD_DIR`. This PR falls back to the managed inbound media directory when the uploads root rejects the requested path, so WebChat attachments are uploadable.

Fixes #83544

AI-assisted: yes. I reviewed the diff and understand the upload path validation change.

## Summary

- **Problem:** `browser.upload` rejects files persisted to `~/.openclaw/media/inbound/` by the WebChat attachment pipeline because upload validation only checks `DEFAULT_UPLOAD_DIR`.
- **Why it matters:** Agent workflows that receive a WebChat file attachment cannot upload that file via the browser tool.
- **What changed:** The upload action now checks the default uploads directory first, then falls back to the managed inbound media directory. Two regression tests were added.
- **What did NOT change (scope boundary):** Arbitrary local paths are still rejected. Both allowed roots go through `resolveExistingPathsWithinRoot`. The `resolveExistingPathsWithinRoot` contract and non-upload browser actions are untouched.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [ ] Gateway / orchestration
- [x] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #83544
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** `browser.upload` now accepts files from `~/.openclaw/media/inbound/` when the default uploads directory rejects the path.
- **Real environment tested:** macOS Darwin 23.6.0, Node v24, local OpenClaw checkout on branch `fix/browser-upload-inbound-media`.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:**

```sh
OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 npx vitest run extensions/browser/src/browser-tool.test.ts --reporter=verbose
```

- **Evidence after fix (terminal output):**

```text
RUN v4.1.6 /Users/zuozheng/Desktop/May Hackthon/openclaw

✓ |extension-browser| browser-tool.test.ts > browser tool upload inbound media fallback (#83544) > accepts files from inbound media directory when uploads directory rejects them
✓ |extension-browser| browser-tool.test.ts > browser tool upload inbound media fallback (#83544) > rejects files outside both uploads and inbound media directories

Test Files 1 passed (1)
Tests 52 passed (52)
Duration 1.31s
```

- **Observed result after fix:** `resolveExistingPathsWithinRoot` is called twice when the upload directory rejects the path: first for uploads, then for inbound media. Files in the inbound media directory resolve successfully; files outside both directories are still rejected.
- **What was not tested:** A full authenticated Gateway/WebChat session with a live model and real file attachment was not tested. This proof covers the upload path-validation logic with focused regression tests.

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- **Root cause:** The upload action in `browser-tool.ts` hardcoded a single allowed root, `DEFAULT_UPLOAD_DIR`. WebChat attachment files are stored in `~/.openclaw/media/inbound/`, but that managed directory was not included in browser upload validation.
- **Missing detection / guardrail:** Existing upload tests mocked path validation as always successful, so they did not cover the rejected-root path.
- **Contributing context (if known):** The default browser upload directory and inbound media directory are owned by different subsystems.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [ ] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- **Target test or file:** `extensions/browser/src/browser-tool.test.ts`
- **Scenario the test should lock in:** Upload accepts files from the inbound media directory when the uploads directory rejects them; upload rejects files outside both allowed directories.
- **Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail:** The tests exercise the exact fallback branch in the upload action without requiring a live browser or Gateway session.
- **Existing test that already covers this (if any):** None.
- **If no new test is added, why not:** N/A.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

WebChat file attachments can now be uploaded to web pages via the `browser.upload` tool when their resolved file path is under `~/.openclaw/media/inbound/`.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Before:
[WebChat attachment] -> media/inbound/report.pdf -> browser.upload
-> resolveExistingPathsWithinRoot(uploads/) -> REJECT

After:
[WebChat attachment] -> media/inbound/report.pdf -> browser.upload
-> resolveExistingPathsWithinRoot(uploads/) -> reject
-> resolveExistingPathsWithinRoot(media/inbound/) -> ACCEPT
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? No
- New/changed network calls? No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? No
- Data access scope changed? Yes
- If any Yes, explain risk + mitigation: `browser.upload` now accepts one additional OpenClaw-managed root, `~/.openclaw/media/inbound/`. The implementation still uses `resolveExistingPathsWithinRoot`, so arbitrary local paths remain rejected.

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: macOS Darwin 23.6.0
- Runtime/container: Node v24
- Model/provider: N/A
- Integration/channel (if any): WebChat attachment path -> browser tool upload validation
- Relevant config (redacted): default OpenClaw config, no custom `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR`

### Steps

1. Use an upload path under `~/.openclaw/media/inbound/`.
2. Call `browser.upload` with that path.
3. Verify the upload action falls back from `DEFAULT_UPLOAD_DIR` to the inbound media directory.

### Expected

The inbound media file is accepted by `browser.upload`.

### Actual

Before this PR, `browser.upload` rejected the inbound media path after checking only `DEFAULT_UPLOAD_DIR`.

## Evidence

Attach at least one:

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [ ] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

## Human Verification (required)

What you personally verified (not just CI), and how:

- **Verified scenarios:** Upload fallback from uploads directory to inbound media directory; rejection of paths outside both directories; existing upload-dir-only behavior remains covered by the existing test suite.
- **Edge cases checked:** Path outside both directories (`/etc/passwd`); upload directory accepts on first try.
- **What you did not verify:** Live WebChat attachment flow with a real Gateway session; Docker sandbox container access to inbound media files.

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? Yes
- Config/env changes? No
- Migration needed? No
- If yes, exact upgrade steps: N/A

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: If a future managed directory needs upload access, the fallback chain must be extended manually.
  - Mitigation: The pattern is localized to the upload action and covered by regression tests.
target.author: zhengzuo0-ai

commands.reason: PR is not opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
